MIGRATION PLAYBOOK
SAP Business Data Cloud · Smart Transitioning Path

From Legacy
to Cloud-Native

The complete guide to SAP BW modernisation — protecting investments while enabling Lift–Shift–Innovate transformation at your own pace

⏳
December 31, 2027 — SAP BW 7.5 exits mainstream maintenance. Extended support to 2030 carries a 2% premium with no new features. After 2030: no patches, no fixes, no safety net. Act strategically now.
Why This Matters Now

Protect Your SAP BW Investments
& Gradually Modernise

With SAP Business Data Cloud (launched February 2025), you can lift your existing SAP BW system to the private cloud, secure your investments, and expose SAP BW data as modern data products — while creating new use cases in SAP Datasphere, Databricks, and any approved third-party application.

The key insight: you do not need a big-bang migration. The Lift–Shift–Innovate methodology allows each phase to deliver immediate value independently, letting you modernise domain by domain at your own pace.

Source: SAP February 2026 · The three-phase BDC modernisation path — protecting BW investments while unlocking cloud-native capabilities
The BW Limitations

Why Traditional BW Cannot
Meet Modern Data Demands

01

Fragmented Data Silos

BW struggles to integrate non-SAP sources, creating isolated information islands across the enterprise.

02

High Operational Costs

On-premises infrastructure demands significant capex, ongoing maintenance, and dedicated IT resource.

03

Batch-Processing Limits

Scheduled batch loads block real-time analytics and immediate decision-making capabilities.

04

Limited AI/ML Support

Legacy BW architecture was not built for ML workloads, generative AI, or intelligent automation.

The Modern Alternative

SAP Business Data Cloud:
Architecture — Today & Future

BDC unifies SAP BW (in private cloud edition) with SAP Datasphere's object store via the Data Product Generator — enabling seamless data product consumption across SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Databricks, and non-SAP sources, all within a single managed contract.

Architecture Overview · SAP BW on-premise (Today) → SAP BW Private Cloud Edition + SAP Datasphere Object Store (Future via BDC)
01

SAP Datasphere

Unified semantic layer — business context, hierarchies, and calculations preserved

02

SAP Analytics Cloud

Integrated planning, visualisation, and augmented analytics in one environment

03

SAP BW Modernisation

Seamless path preserving all existing BW investments and business logic

04

SAP Databricks

Advanced AI/ML and data engineering via Delta Share and flattening operators

Three-Phase Methodology

The Migration Blueprint:
Lift — Shift — Innovate

A proven, risk-managed approach. Each phase is independent — delivering immediate value without waiting for full modernisation.

PHASE 01

LIFT

Move SAP BW as-is to SAP-managed private cloud. Zero disruption. Maintenance to 2030.

  • No conversion to BW/4HANA required*
  • Cloud migration support incl. DMIS
  • All BW features preserved
  • Removes hardware maintenance
  • Lowers TCO immediately
PHASE 02

SHIFT

Expose BW data as Data Products via the Data Product Generator. Enable new use cases.

  • Direct access to Datasphere object store
  • ML/AI use cases via Databricks
  • Data transformation & modelling
  • Zero-copy via Delta Share
  • Self-service analytics enabled
PHASE 03

INNOVATE

Replace BW workflows with SAP-managed Data Products and Intelligent Applications.

  • Standardised public cloud releases
  • SAP ERP clean core strategy
  • Lakehouse architecture adoption
  • Open partner ecosystem
  • Decommission BW gradually
⏱ Typical Lift duration: 3–6 months · Shift & Innovate proceed domain-by-domain at your own pace · *Relevant for BW NetWeaver context
Phase 1 Deep-Dive

Decision Tree: Which Lift
Path Is Right for You?

Your migration route depends on your current SAP BW version and database. The decision tree below — drawn directly from SAP's February 2026 guidance — maps every starting point to the correct cloud path.

SAP BDC Decision Tree · From SAP BW NetWeaver & SAP BW/4HANA to private cloud edition — every route mapped

From SAP BW NetWeaver

1

Migrate to SAP HANA Any DB → SAP HANA migration

2

Ensure BW 7.5 on HANA Baseline version for cloud path

3

Update to SPS 24+ Latest available: SPS 31

4

Lift to SAP BW, Private Cloud Edition

From SAP BW/4HANA

1

Upgrade to BW/4HANA 2023 From 1.0 or 2.0

2

Update to SPS 04+ From BW/4HANA 2021

3

Update to latest SPS 11 From BW/4HANA 2023

4

Lift to BW/4HANA, Private Cloud Edition

Alternative Paths
🔼 Lift first, convert later
🔄 In-place conversion
🚀 Shell / remote conversion
🌱 Greenfield (fresh start)
Phase 2 Deep-Dive

SHIFT: BDC's Business
Data Fabric & Zero-Copy Access

The SHIFT phase exposes proven SAP BW data as modern Data Products via the Data Product Generator — enabling direct consumption in SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Databricks, and third-party tools, all without duplicating data.

BDC Business Data Fabric · Zero-copy replication from SAP BW private cloud → Datasphere Object Store → SAP Databricks via Delta Share

Supported InfoProviders for Data Product Generation

InfoObject · (a)DSO · CompositeProvider · MultiProvider · InfoCube · Query as InfoProvider — all supported with delta load scheduling for incremental updates.

Data Product Layering · BW InfoProvider → Data Product Generator → Delta Table in Datasphere Object Store → Delta Share for SAP Databricks
Component Translation

BW to BDC Artifact Mapping

Every BW on-premise component has a clear, modern counterpart in SAP Business Data Cloud. The Data Product Generator bridges BW objects to cloud data products, enabling a gradual, risk-free transition.

SAP BW On-Premise SAP Business Data Cloud
Query→SAC Story / Query Template Generator
CompositeProvider / MultiProvider→Analytic Model / View
InfoObject / aDSO / InfoCube / DSO→Local Tables (File)
DTP / Transformation→Transformation Flow
Standard DataSource→SAP-managed Data Product
DataSource / Replication Flow→Remote Table / SAP-managed Data Product
Source Systems (SAP ECC, S/4HANA)→S/4HANA RISE/PCE / Foundation Service
Key principle: Replacing with BW Data Products first (via Data Product Generator), then gradually replacing Standard DataSources with SAP-managed Data Products and translating BW Transformations into Datasphere Transformation Flows.
BW Object Migration Reference

BW Object-by-Object
Migration Decision Guide

Each BW object follows a specific migration approach depending on its architectural role. The table below — cross-verified against SAP's February 2026 guidance and the Data Product Generator specification — gives architects and consultants a precise decision reference for each object type.

⚠️

Approach Verification Note: All approach designations below have been cross-checked against SAP BDC documentation. One nuance applies to CompositeProvider — while its full replacement targets the Innovate phase, the Data Product Generator also supports CompositeProvider for interim data exposure during the Shift phase (enhanced delta capability arriving Q1 2026). Both paths are shown.

BW Object Role in BW BDC Equivalent Approach Key Constraint
ADSO Persistent fact / transactional store Local Table (File) + Views / Analytic Model Shift via DPG DPG supports delta/full load; HDLFS storage in Datasphere object store ✅ Confirmed
InfoCube Star-schema fact table Local Table (File) + modeled semantics Shift via DPG DPG explicitly supports InfoCubes; delta load scheduling available ✅ Confirmed
CompositeProvider Virtual model (joins / unions) Datasphere Views / Analytic Models Shift via DPG (interim)
Innovate (full replace) 		DPG supports CompositeProvider for data exposure (enhanced delta Q1 2026). Full replacement via Views/Analytic Models is Innovate. ⚠️ Dual-path
BW Query Semantic / reporting runtime Datasphere Analytic Model + SAC Story Innovate Query Template Generator planned Q2 2026. ABAP customer exits not translated 1:1 — manual rework required ✅ Confirmed
Process Chains Batch orchestration Keep in BW initially; replace with Datasphere task chains Lift → Shift Retained during Lift phase. Task chain ops API is the innovation focus for progressive replacement ✅ Confirmed
Extractors Data acquisition into BW SAP-managed Data Products (CDS-based) Re-platform Standard DataSources replaced by SAP-managed Data Products; data re-routed via S/4HANA RISE/PCE + Foundation Service ✅ Confirmed
Approach Legend
Shift via DPG Data Product Generator exposes BW InfoProviders to Datasphere object store — immediate value, no BW rework
Innovate Full replacement with native BDC constructs (Views, Analytic Models, SAC Stories) — clean core target
Lift → Shift Retain in BW Private Cloud during Lift, then progressively replace with Datasphere equivalents
Re-platform Replace data acquisition layer entirely — Standard DataSources → SAP-managed CDS-based Data Products
Advanced Shift Capability

Flattening Operator for
SAP Databricks ML/AI Scenarios

A dedicated Flattening Operator in Datasphere Transformation Flows enables selection of associated attributes with transactional BW data — creating optimised flat Delta Tables for machine learning and AI workloads in SAP Databricks.

Derived Data Products — Flattening Operator (Preview) · InfoProvider → Data Product Generator → Delta Table → Spark Transformation → Flattened Delta Table → SAP Databricks via Delta Share
Step 1 — Push InfoProviders

Make BW InfoProviders (including associations) available in the Datasphere object store via Data Product Generator

Step 2 — Transformation Flow

Share the table and integrate into a Spark Transformation Flow in a custom Datasphere space

Step 3 — Flatten the Data

Select required associated attributes and transactional data to create a flattened table via the Flattening Operator

Step 4 — Expose via Delta Share

Expose the resulting flattened table as a Data Product via Delta Share for consumption in SAP Databricks

Phase 3 Deep-Dive

INNOVATE: Query Template Generator
& Intelligent Applications

The Query Template Generator (planned Q2 2026) pushes BW query metadata into SAP BDC as Analytic Model templates — dramatically reducing rebuild effort during the Innovate phase. Query structure, fact sources, and reusable dimensions are all transferred and translated automatically.

Query Template Generator (Preview) · BW Query + InfoProvider → Analytic Model incl. Fact View and reusable Dimensions in SAP Datasphere
Transition with Generators · Step 1: Lift → Step 2: Data Product Generator + Query Template Generator → Step 3: Replace BW use cases with Data Products & Intelligent Apps
Architecture Evolution

Adopt BDC at Your Own Pace:
Three Architecture States

SAP BDC adoption follows a natural progression through three architectural states — each building on the previous while maintaining full business continuity. The target picture decommissions SAP BW only when all use cases are confidently migrated.

State 1 — Getting Started · BW Private Cloud Edition feeds BW Data Products into the Inbound Layer. Analytic Models and Views built in Datasphere. 1:1 BW history preserved in Databricks.
State 2 — At Your Own Pace · BW Data Products co-exist with SAP-managed Data Products. SAP S/4HANA introduced as additional source. Modernisation proceeds domain by domain.
State 3 — Target Picture · SAP BW decommissioned. All data flows from SAP S/4HANA via SAP-managed Data Products. Full BDC-native landscape achieved.
Innovate Detail

Replacing with SAP-Managed
Data Products & Intelligent Applications

The final Innovate step replaces BW workflows systematically. Queries and CompositeProviders give way to Stories and Analytic Models in Intelligent Applications. DataSources are replaced by SAP-managed Data Products with data re-routed via S/4HANA RISE/PCE and Foundation Service.

Innovate Replacement Pattern · BW Query/CompositeProvider → Intelligent Application (Story + Analytic Model + View) · DataSource → SAP-managed Data Product via Foundation Service
Reporting Layer

Query and CompositeProvider replaced by Story, Analytic Model, and View within an Intelligent Application

Data Foundation Layer

DataSources replaced with SAP-managed Data Products; data provisioning re-routed to S/4HANA RISE/PCE

AI at the Core

Joule & Intelligent Applications

SAP's AI copilot Joule is embedded across BDC — providing natural language access to enterprise data with intelligent automation. Combined with 40+ AI engines and SAP Databricks, the Innovate phase unlocks powerful AI-driven analytics across Finance, Supply Chain, HR, and Procurement.

💬
Natural Language Queries

Ask questions in plain English, get instant answers from enterprise data

🧠
Contextual AI Insights

AI understands SAP business context for relevant, accurate analytical results

⚙️
40+ AI Engines

Integrated with leading AI models and frameworks including SAP Databricks

📊
Deep Research

Strategic reporting drawing from SAP and external data sources combined

Role-Based AI Agents

Finance Agent

Automates accruals, cash management, and financial analysis workflows — 70% effort reduction

Supply Chain Agent

Production planning, inventory optimisation, demand forecasting — 25% productivity gain

HR Agent

Talent development, performance management, and workforce planning automation

Procurement Agent

Bid analysis, supplier evaluation, and contract management with AI-driven insights

SAP BDC Roadmap 2026

Business Warehouse Modernisation
Roadmap — What's Coming

SAP BDC Official Roadmap (Preview — subject to change) · Q1/Q2 2026 feature delivery for BW Modernisation
✅ Already Delivered

Data Product Generator: Supports InfoObject, (a)DSO, CompositeProvider, MultiProvider, InfoCube, Query as InfoProvider · Multiple Datasphere targets and spaces · Mass handling and semantic import of BW 7.5 and BW/4HANA data models incl. associations

Q1 2026
Enhanced Delta Capability

Enable delta load for CompositeProvider and MultiProvider when PartProviders are loaded via delta DTP

Q1 2026
Custom Technical Name

Provide a custom technical name for subscriptions in the Data Product Generator

Q2 2026
Creation of Primary Data Products

Push of primary data products via Data Product Generator for broad consumption

Q2 2026
Flattening Operator

Dedicated operator in Transformation Flows for ML/AI scenarios in SAP Databricks

Q2 2026
Query Template Generator

Push query metadata as reusable templates into SAP Business Data Cloud

Q2 2026
Hierarchy Support in DPG

Include hierarchy semantics when transferring BW data to Datasphere object store

Q2 2026
BW Entities in Data Product Studio

Access local table (files) based on BW InfoProviders to create customer-managed data products
